# opsy_seristack (Action)

Seristack is a task automation tool .Action block for stack execution will run specific stack from the list of stacks in seristack.yaml.
To know more about seristack, please visit [seristack](https://seristack.getsaas.in/).

Below sample seristack.yaml file should be added in the root path from where terraform cmds are executed.


```yaml
#seristack.yaml
stacks:
- name: welcome
  count: 1
  cmds:
  - |
    echo "welcome {{.Vars.msg}}-{{.Count.index}}"
- name: bye
  count: 1
  cmds:
  - |
    echo "bye {{.Vars.msg}}-{{.Count.index}}"
```

```terraform
action "opsy_seristack" "action1" {
  config {
    configfile = "${path.module}/seristack.yaml"
    stackname  = "welcome"
    vars = {
      msg = "hello"
    }
  }
}

action "opsy_seristack" "action2" {
  config {
    configfile = "${path.module}/seristack.yaml"
    stackname  = "bye"
    vars = {
      msg = "hello"
    }
  }
}
```

```bash
terraform apply -invoke=action.opsy_seristack.action1

terraform apply -invoke=action.opsy_seristack.action2
```


<!-- action schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `configfile` (String) Configfuration file
- `stackname` (String) Stack name to be executed

### Optional

- `vars` (Map of String) Variables to pass to the stack
